main_sections.title: Best Friends
main_sections.prose_summary: Four geometric shape-friends — Circle, Square, Triangle, and Rectangle — start arguing about who is the best. Circle boasts of being round and beautiful with no sharp edges. Square proudly claims its four equal sides and its presence in sweets. Triangle asserts its beauty with three sides and its use in drawing mountains and snacks. Rectangle laughs and insists that its two long and two short sides make it the best, pointing to biscuits and chocolates. Unable to resolve the dispute, they agree to ask the first person they meet — a star. The wise star gently reminds them that they are all friends, and that together they can create the most beautiful pictures and patterns. She invites them to look around and notice how every shape has its own value. The friends realise their mistake, promise never to fight again, and remain best friends forever. The story carries themes of friendship, collaboration, acceptance of differences, and the value of each individual.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Answer the following.
1. Who were the four friends?
2. Why were they fighting?
3. Who helped them to end their fight?
4. What lesson did the friends learn in the end?
5. Write 'T' for true and 'F' for false.
a) The circle does not have sharp edges.
b) A square has four sides but not all of them are equal in size.
c) The triangle has two sides.
d) The friends could decide who was the best among them.

question_bank.stem: Who were the four friends?
Why were they fighting?
Who helped them to end their fight?
What lesson did the friends learn in the end?
The circle does not have sharp edges.
A square has four sides but not all of them are equal in size.
The triangle has two sides.
The friends could decide who was the best among them.

oracy.section_name: Let us think — items 6–7 + Let us speak

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
6. Who is your friend? What are the things you can do when your friend helps you?
7. Look around you. Talk about the shapes of what you see.

question_bank.stem: Who is your friend? What are the things you can do when your friend helps you?
Look around you. Talk about the shapes of what you see.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Divide into groups of 5. Each of you will be one character in the story — circle, square, triangle, rectangle and the star. Take turns and read out the story. Enjoy by adding actions to what you say.

question_bank.stem: In groups of 5, role-play the story: each student takes the part of circle, square, triangle, rectangle, or the star and reads their character's lines with actions.

word_work.section_name: Let us learn — A + B + C

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Fill in the blanks using 'a'.
1. ___ dog is barking at the postman.
2. My mother gives chapattis to ___ cow everyday.
3. In summers, ___ sparrow builds its nest on the mango tree near our house.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blank with 'a': ___ dog is barking at the postman.
Fill in the blank with 'a': My mother gives chapattis to ___ cow everyday.
Fill in the blank with 'a': In summers, ___ sparrow builds its nest on the mango tree near our house.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Fill in the blanks using 'an'.
1. I saw ___ eagle yesterday.
2. Maya bought ___ umbrella for the monsoon.
3. ___ ice-cream man brings his cart in the evenings.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blank with 'an': I saw ___ eagle yesterday.
Fill in the blank with 'an': Maya bought ___ umbrella for the monsoon.
Fill in the blank with 'an': ___ ice-cream man brings his cart in the evenings.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
C. Fill in the blanks using 'a' or 'an'.
1. ___ brown hen laid ___ egg.
2. ___ eagle sat on ___ building.
3. Mary ate ___ apricot, ___ chikoo and ___ orange.

question_bank.stem: Fill in 'a' or 'an': ___ brown hen laid ___ egg.
Fill in 'a' or 'an': ___ eagle sat on ___ building.
Fill in 'a' or 'an': Mary ate ___ apricot, ___ chikoo and ___ orange.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: A. Paste a picture of your friend.

B. Write a few lines about your friend by filling in the blanks.
My Friend
1. My friend's name is ___.
2. He/She is a boy/girl.
3. He/She is ___ years old.
4. His/Her favourite colour is ___.
5. He/She likes to play ___.
6. He/She wants to become ___.
I love my friend.

question_bank.stem: My friend's name is ___.
He/She is ___ years old.
His/Her favourite colour is ___.

beyond_text.section_name: Let us do — A + Let us explore — A

tasks_verbatim.task_text: A. Use the space given below to draw the four friends you met in Chapter 3.
A. Draw pictures using the four friends mentioned in Chapter 3. You can use the pictures given below for ideas.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Illustration of a cityscape built entirely from geometric shapes — triangles as mountains and rooftops, rectangles as buildings, circles as the sun and trees, with two shape-people standing in the foreground — provided as inspiration for learners' own drawing.
